mnemonics.title: PIPED C-F-B
mnemonics.content: PIPED C-F-B stands for: Process management, Input/Output management, Performance, Efficiency, Deadlock prevention, Communication, File management, Budget (resource allocation). These are the key functions of an Operating System.

mnemonics.title: My Very Educated Mother Just Served Us Nine Pizzas
mnemonics.content: For OS Structure types: Monolithic, Virtual Machine, Extensible, Microkernel, Uniprocessor, Jini, Structured, User services, Nine (Network), Pizzas (Process management). Actually: M-V-E-M-P-U-S-N-P: Monolithic, Virtual, Extensible, Microkernel, Uniprocessor, Structured, Network, Personal computer systems.

mnemonics.title: New Delhi Police Maintain Law And Order
mnemonics.content: For OS Operations: New (New/Ready state), Delhi (Deadlock prevention), Police (Process management), Maintain (Memory management), Law (Logical I/O), And (Application interface), Order (Output management).

acronyms.usage: The fundamental system software that acts as an intermediary between user and computer hardware
The primary component that executes instructions; managed by OS through scheduling
Visual interface provided by OS for user interaction (icons, windows, menus)
Set of functions OS provides for application programs to request services
Firmware that initializes hardware before OS loads; lowest level of system software
Visual way to interact with OS using icons, windows, and menus
Mechanism for processes to communicate; a key OS operation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an onion with layers: innermost is hardware, then kernel (core), then system calls, then user interface, outermost is applications. Each layer only interacts with adjacent layers.

Visualize a traffic signal system: New (green light just turned on), Ready (amber - waiting to go), Running (green - go), Waiting (red - stopped), Terminated (light off). Processes move between these states like vehicles at intersections.
Picture a hotel manager: CPU is the front desk, memory is rooms, I/O devices are facilities (pool, gym). The manager (OS) allocates and deallocates resources to guests (programs) efficiently.

Monolithic = a big factory where everything happens under one roof. Microkernel = a small coordinator who hires specialists (servers) for different jobs and only handles essential communication between them.

analogies.analogy: Think of OS like a government - it doesn't directly produce anything but provides essential services (defense, transportation, utilities) so citizens (application programs) can function productively. It makes decisions about resource allocation and maintains order.

Like a restaurant manager scheduling tables and staff during peak hours. The manager (CPU scheduler) decides which customer (process) gets served when, ensuring all get attention while maximizing efficiency.

Like a library librarian managing book loans. Just as the librarian tracks who has which book and ensures returns, OS tracks memory allocation to processes and frees it when done. It also handles 'book reservations' (memory protection) so one person doesn't take all books.

Like a filing cabinet manager who organizes, stores, retrieves, and protects documents. The manager creates folders (directories), ensures only authorized people can access sensitive files, and maintains an index for quick retrieval.

Like an air traffic controller managing takeoffs and landings. The controller (OS) allocates runways (I/O devices) to aircraft (processes), handles priority emergencies, and ensures smooth traffic flow without collisions.
